title: Daily Briefing — Apr 14
headline: Tech keeps grinding higher, but sector rotation is sneaking in under the surface.
body: Right, so let's look at what the flows are telling us. Markets had a broad green session — SPY +1.0% to 686.10, QQQ +1.0%, with Tech once again holding the leadership baton (XLK +2.1%, approaching the 20 MA at 136.95). Bonds (HYG/LQD/TLT) all ticked up, so no immediate macro alarm bells — if bonds freak out, everything breaks, and they're not today.

Rotation watch: Tech (XLK/XLC) remains in the leading quadrant with strong momentum, but Industrials (XLI), Energy (XLE), and Materials (XLB) are losing steam (weakening quadrant). Defensive rotation hasn’t fully triggered yet, but Healthcare (XLV, +0.5%) and Financials (XLF, +1.8%) are both improving — watch for mean reversion plays into these lagging sectors as they approach their 20 MAs.

Contrarian lens: Fear & Greed bouncing off lows (42.5, up from 21.4 a month ago) — we're rising out of Extreme Fear, so the easy mean reversion trade is fading. No Smart Money/Dumb Money signal tripped, but don't get complacent on crowded Tech. Less trades = more wins right now.
callout_body:
SPY near 20 MA: 659.00 — bulls in control above
XLK at 145.61, well over 20 MA (136.95)
Watch XLF for follow-through above 50.79 (SMA50)
Healthcare (XLV) mean reversion zone: SMA20 = 146.68
VIX: 19.16 — below regime lines, risk appetite returning
Bonds (HYG/LQD) both approaching, not breaking — macro safe for now
action_items: Stay long Tech (XLK, QQQ) above their 20 MAs, but trail stops and don't chase runaways like DELL (+6.7%), ARM (+5.8%), NBIS (+6.6%) — abundance mindset.
Watch Healthcare (XLV, SYK, TMO, GKOS) and Financials (XLF) for rotation entries if they hold above their SMA20s, as sector rotation could start favouring laggards.
closing_quote: Remember: If it's in the press, it's in the price. Let the flows talk, not the headlines.
